user.title: PowerSync setup
user.description: Configure PowerSync to sync electricity pricing to your battery system automatically.

provider_selection.title: Electricity provider
provider_selection.description: Select your electricity retailer. PowerSync will fetch pricing and sync it to your battery on a 5-minute cycle.

data_description.electricity_provider: If your retailer is not listed, choose Other / Custom TOU to enter rates manually.

flow_power_setup.title: Flow Power pricing
flow_power_setup.description: Enter your base rate from your Flow Power bill.

data_description.flow_power_state: Your NEM region determines which wholesale price feed is used. Check your bill if unsure.
data_description.flow_power_base_rate: The base energy rate from your Flow Power contract, before wholesale adjustments.

flow_power_tariff.title: Network tariff
flow_power_tariff.description: Select your network tariff for accurate PEA pricing. Leave as None to use the simple formula.

data_description.fp_network_tariff_combined: Your DNSP and tariff code combined (e.g. 'Ausgrid — RESELE'). Filtered by your NEM region. Select None to use the simple formula.

flow_power_portal.title: Flow Power portal
flow_power_portal.description: Connect your portal account to get actual billing data (PEA, LWAP, TWAP, DLF) instead of estimates. Requires SMS verification.

data_description.connect_portal: When enabled, PowerSync fetches real billing components from your Flow Power account. Disable to use estimated pricing only.

flow_power_portal_login.title: Portal login
flow_power_portal_login.description: Enter your Flow Power portal credentials. An SMS code will be sent to your registered phone.

data_description.flowpower_email: The email address you use to log in to the Flow Power portal.
data_description.flowpower_password: Your Flow Power portal password. Stored securely in Home Assistant.

flow_power_portal_mfa.title: SMS verification
flow_power_portal_mfa.description: Enter the SMS code sent to your registered phone number.

data_description.mfa_code: 6-digit code from the SMS sent to your Flow Power registered phone. Codes expire after 5 minutes.

amber.title: Amber Electric API key
amber.description: Connect your Amber account so PowerSync can read live pricing. Prices update every 5 minutes.

data_description.amber_api_token: Found in the Amber app under Profile > Developer > API key. Or visit [{amber_url}]({amber_url}).

localvolts.title: Localvolts API credentials
localvolts.description: Connect your Localvolts account for real-time 5-minute NEM wholesale pricing.

data_description.localvolts_api_key: Contact Localvolts support to request your API key. Keep it secret -- treat it like a password.
data_description.localvolts_partner_id: Your Localvolts Partner ID, provided alongside the API key.
data_description.localvolts_nmi: Your 10-digit National Meter Identifier (on your electricity bill). Enter * to authorize all NMIs on the account.

amber_settings.title: Amber pricing options
amber_settings.description: Configure spike protection, export boost, and chip mode for Amber pricing.

data_description.spike_protection_enabled: When Amber detects a price spike, artificially inflate buy prices to prevent your battery from charging from grid.
data_description.settled_prices_only: Skip forecast sync at :00 and only sync when actual prices arrive at :35/:60. Use when forecasts differ significantly from settled prices.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_alert: Send a mobile notification when predicted and conservative forecasts diverge significantly. Indicates the forecast may be unreliable.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_threshold: Average price difference that triggers the alert. Default 10 c/kWh.
data_description.force_tariff_mode_toggle: After uploading a new tariff, briefly toggle operation mode to force immediate recalculation. Tesla only, causes a ~5 second interruption.
data_description.export_boost_enabled: Artificially increase sell prices during configured hours to encourage battery export during high-value periods.
data_description.export_price_offset: Amount added to the actual Amber export price. Example: Amber 18c + Offset 5c = battery sees 23c.
data_description.export_min_price: Floor price after offset. Example: Amber 12c + Offset 5c = 17c, bumped to Floor 20c.
data_description.export_boost_start: Start time for the export boost window (24-hour format).
data_description.export_boost_end: End time for the export boost window (24-hour format).
data_description.export_boost_threshold: Only apply boost when the actual Amber price (before boost) is at or above this value. Use 0 to always boost.
data_description.chip_mode_enabled: Suppress exports during configured hours unless price exceeds threshold. The inverse of export boost.
data_description.chip_mode_start: Start time for the chip mode window (24-hour format).
data_description.chip_mode_end: End time for the chip mode window (24-hour format).
data_description.chip_mode_threshold: Allow exports only when the price is at or above this value during chip mode hours.

aemo_config.title: AEMO spike detection
aemo_config.description: When AEMO wholesale prices spike above your threshold, PowerSync will switch your battery to export mode to earn spike credits.

data_description.aemo_spike_enabled: Required for Globird and AEMO VPP plans that include spike export payments. Disable if your plan does not pay for spike exports.
data_description.aemo_region: Your NEM region determines which AEMO price feed is monitored. Check your electricity bill if unsure.
data_description.aemo_spike_threshold: Battery exports when the AEMO wholesale price exceeds this value. Default $3,000/MWh matches most Globird and VPP plans. Only change if your plan specifies a different threshold.

epex.title: EPEX day-ahead pricing
epex.description: Configure European day-ahead pricing. Prices are sourced from ENTSO-E via the EPEX Predictor API (free, no account needed).

data_description.epex_region: Your EPEX electricity market bidding zone. Most users need just their country code (DE, AT, BE, NL, FR, etc.).
data_description.epex_surcharge: Fixed network fees, levies, and other charges in ct/kWh. Added to the wholesale price before tax.
data_description.epex_tax_percent: VAT percentage applied on top of the total price. Example: 21 for Belgium, 19 for Germany.
data_description.epex_export_rate: Fixed feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Set to 0 to use the wholesale price for export valuation.

octopus.title: Octopus Energy tariff
octopus.description: Select your Octopus product and GSP region. Find your GSP region on your Octopus bill or at {octopus_url}.

data_description.octopus_product: Agile = half-hourly dynamic. Go/Intelligent Go = cheap overnight EV rates. Flux/Intelligent Flux = solar+battery optimised. Tracker = daily wholesale.
data_description.octopus_region: Your Grid Supply Point region (single letter A-P). Check your Octopus bill or account dashboard.

octopus_saving_sessions.title: Saving sessions
octopus_saving_sessions.description: PowerSync automatically exports your battery during Octopus Saving Sessions to earn Octopoints. Connect via Octopus API or the Bottlecap Dave integration.

data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_enabled: Enable automatic battery export during Octopus Saving Sessions.
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_source: Direct uses the Octopus GraphQL API (requires API key). Entity reads from Bottlecap Dave's Octopus integration.
data_description.octopus_api_key: Your Octopus API key from the developer dashboard (not the same as your public tariff API key).
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_entity: Entity ID of the Bottlecap Dave saving sessions event entity (e.g. event.octopus_energy_saving_sessions).
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_auto_join: Automatically join available saving sessions. Only works in direct mode.

data_description.skip_tariff: Check to use default estimation instead of configuring custom rates.
data_description.plan_name: Optional label for this tariff plan. Shown in the integration card.
data_description.tariff_type: Flat rate uses a single price all day. Time of Use lets you add multiple periods with different rates.
data_description.flat_rate: Only used for flat rate tariff type.
data_description.offpeak_rate: Rate for hours not covered by any period you define.
data_description.fit_rate: Default export rate for off-peak hours.

data_description.period_type: e.g. Peak, Shoulder, Off-Peak, Super Off-Peak.
data_description.period_days: Which days this period applies to (weekdays, weekends, or all).
data_description.import_rate: What you pay per kWh to import during this period.
data_description.export_rate: What you earn per kWh for exporting during this period.
data_description.add_another: Uncheck when done. Remaining hours become off-peak.

nz_retailer.title: NZ electricity retailer
nz_retailer.description: Select your retailer and distribution zone. Rates will be pre-filled and can be adjusted in the next step.

data_description.nz_retailer: Your NZ electricity retailer. Rates are pre-filled from the selected plan.
data_description.nz_distribution_zone: Your local network distribution zone. Check your electricity bill if unsure.

nz_rates.title: NZ electricity rates
nz_rates.description: Adjust rates to match your actual plan. All values are in NZD cents per kWh.

data_description.nz_shoulder_rate: Weekday daytime and weekend daytime rate.
data_description.nz_daily_supply: Fixed daily supply charge from your retailer.

battery_system.title: Battery system
battery_system.description: Select the battery or hybrid inverter installed at your site. This determines which connection protocol PowerSync uses.

data_description.battery_system: If you have multiple battery brands, choose the primary system that PowerSync should control.

optimization_provider.title: Optimization mode
optimization_provider.description: Choose how PowerSync schedules your battery. The built-in option uses your {battery_name}'s native TOU scheduler; Smart Optimization runs a cost-minimising LP solver inside PowerSync.

data_description.optimization_provider: Smart Optimization generally delivers lower bills but requires scipy. The native mode is simpler and uses your battery's own scheduling logic.

ml_options.title: Smart Optimization settings
ml_options.description: Set the minimum discharge level for the LP optimizer. This is the optimizer's floor, not your battery's hardware backup reserve for grid outages.

data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will never discharge below this percentage. Default 20% is a good starting point. Your battery's hardware backup reserve (set in the Tesla/Sigenergy app) is separate.

sungrow.title: Sungrow Modbus connection
sungrow.description: Connect to your Sungrow SH-series hybrid inverter via Modbus TCP.

data_description.sungrow_host: Local IP of your Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sungrow_port: Default Modbus TCP port is 502. Only change if customised on the inverter.
data_description.sungrow_slave_id: Default is 1. Only change if you have multiple devices on the same bus.

sungrow_secondary.title: Secondary Sungrow inverter
sungrow_secondary.description: If you have a second Sungrow SH inverter, enter its connection details. Leave the IP blank to skip.

data_description.sungrow_host_2: Local IP of your second Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle. Leave blank to skip.
data_description.sungrow_slave_id_2: Default is 1. Only change if customised.

foxess_connection.title: FoxESS connection type
foxess_connection.description: Choose how to connect to your FoxESS inverter.

data_description.foxess_connection_type: Modbus TCP connects over LAN/WiFi. RS485 Serial provides full register access via a direct serial adapter.

foxess_tcp.title: FoxESS Modbus TCP
foxess_tcp.description: Connect to your FoxESS inverter over the local network. The model family will be auto-detected.

data_description.foxess_host: Local IP of your FoxESS inverter. H3 Smart may appear as 'espressif' on your network.

foxess_serial.title: FoxESS RS485 serial
foxess_serial.description: Connect to your FoxESS inverter via RS485 serial for full register access including BMS data.

data_description.foxess_serial_baudrate: Default 9600. Only change if the inverter is configured differently.

foxess_model.title: Confirm FoxESS model
foxess_model.description: Connection successful. Please confirm your inverter model.

data_description.foxess_model_family: H3-Pro and H3 Smart use the same register set. The H3 Smart has built-in WiFi Modbus.

foxess_cloud.title: FoxESS Cloud (optional)
foxess_cloud.description: Optionally connect FoxESS Cloud to sync prices as time-based schedules. Not required for battery control. Leave blank to skip.

data_description.foxess_cloud_api_key: Get your API key from foxesscloud.com > User Centre > API Management. Leave blank to skip.
data_description.foxess_cloud_device_sn: Inverter serial number from FoxESS Cloud. Leave blank to auto-detect.

goodwe_connection.title: GoodWe inverter connection
goodwe_connection.description: Connect to your GoodWe hybrid inverter. The model will be auto-detected.

data_description.goodwe_host: Local IP address of your GoodWe inverter.
data_description.goodwe_protocol: UDP for WiFi dongles (port 8899), TCP for LAN dongles (port 502).

esy_sunhome.title: ESY Sunhome connection
esy_sunhome.description: PowerSync bridges through the ESY Sunhome integration. Select the ESY Sunhome config entry to use.

data_description.esy_config_entry_id: The ESY Sunhome integration entry to read sensors and send mode commands through. Install it via HACS if it does not appear here.

sigenergy_credentials.title: Sigenergy Cloud login
sigenergy_credentials.description: Enter your Sigenergy Cloud API credentials to connect your system.

data_description.sigenergy_username: Your Sigenergy Cloud account email address.
data_description.sigenergy_device_id: 13-digit device ID. Only needed if authentication fails without it. Find it in browser dev tools > Network tab > 'token' request > userDeviceId.
data_description.sigenergy_pass_enc: Pre-encoded password from the Sigenergy API. Leave blank unless you know what this is.

sigenergy_station.title: Sigenergy station
sigenergy_station.description: Select your Sigenergy station. If the list is unavailable, enter the ID manually.

data_description.sigenergy_station_id: To find your Station ID, ask SigenAI 'Tell me my StationID' in the Sigenergy app.

sigenergy_modbus.title: Sigenergy Modbus connection
sigenergy_modbus.description: Configure Modbus TCP to read real-time solar, battery, and grid power data from your Sigenergy system.

data_description.sigenergy_modbus_host: Local IP address of your Sigenergy inverter or gateway (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_port: Default Modbus TCP port is 502. Only change if customised.
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_slave_id: Default is 247. PowerSync auto-detects the correct registers.

sigenergy_dc_curtailment.title: DC solar curtailment
sigenergy_dc_curtailment.description: When enabled, PowerSync limits DC solar production during negative prices via Modbus. For AC-coupled inverters, configure curtailment separately.

data_description.sigenergy_dc_curtailment_enabled: Reduce DC solar output when export prices go negative. Requires Modbus write access to the Sigenergy system.
data_description.sigenergy_export_limit_kw: Your DNSP grid export limit in kW. Leave blank to auto-detect from inverter registers. Prevents PowerSync from ever exceeding your grid connection allowance.

tesla_provider.title: Tesla API connection
tesla_provider.description:
{fleet_detected}

Choose how PowerSync connects to your Tesla account for Powerwall control, and optionally for vehicle commands.

data_description.tesla_api_provider: Controls how PowerSync reads and writes your Powerwall TOU schedule. PowerSync.cc is free and handles OAuth for you.
data_description.tesla_ev_api_provider: Only needed if you want PowerSync to send commands to a Tesla vehicle (e.g. start/stop charging). Choose None if you only have a Powerwall or use BLE.

tesla_ev_teslemetry_token.title: Teslemetry vehicle token
tesla_ev_teslemetry_token.description: The Teslemetry HA integration is not installed, so PowerSync needs a token to send vehicle commands directly.

data_description.tesla_ev_teslemetry_token: Get a token from [{teslemetry_url}]({teslemetry_url}). This is only used for vehicle commands -- your Powerwall uses a separate connection.

teslemetry.title: Teslemetry API key
teslemetry.description: Enter your Teslemetry API token to connect PowerSync to your Tesla account via the Teslemetry proxy (~$4/month).

data_description.teslemetry_api_token: Get a token from teslemetry.com. Paste the full token string.

powersync.title: Sign in with PowerSync
powersync.description: Sign in with your Tesla account at the link below. You will receive a token starting with `psync_` -- paste it here.

data_description.teslemetry_api_token: Open {auth_url} in a browser, sign in with Tesla, and copy the psync_ token. Your Tesla credentials are never shared with PowerSync.

powersync_reauth.title: Re-authenticate with PowerSync
powersync_reauth.description: Your PowerSync token is no longer valid. Sign in again at **{auth_url}** and paste the new `psync_` token below.

teslemetry_reauth.title: Re-authenticate Teslemetry
teslemetry_reauth.description: Your Teslemetry token is no longer valid. Get a new one from teslemetry.com and paste it below.

site_selection.title: Select your sites
site_selection.description: Match your Tesla energy site with your electricity account. If you only have one of each, they are pre-selected.

data.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop export at negative feed-in prices

data_description.tesla_energy_site_id: The Powerwall or Solar site that PowerSync will control. If you have multiple Tesla sites, choose the one at this address.
data_description.amber_site_id: If you have multiple Amber NMIs, select the one associated with this Powerwall.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: When enabled, PowerSync uploads a new TOU tariff schedule to your battery every 5 minutes based on current Amber prices.
data_description.amber_forecast_type: Predicted is balanced. Low assumes cheaper prices (more aggressive charging). High assumes expensive prices (more conservative).
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Prevents your battery from exporting when feed-in prices go negative, so you never pay to export. Can be fine-tuned in options later.

weather_setup.title: Weather & solar forecast
weather_setup.description: Configure weather automations and solar forecasting. If Solcast HA integration is already installed, PowerSync detects it automatically.

data_description.weather_location: Suburb, city, postcode, or lat,lon coordinates (e.g. 'Melbourne', '3000', '-37.81,144.96'). Leave blank to auto-detect from timezone.
data_description.openweathermap_api_key: Free API key from openweathermap.org for weather-based automations (sunny/cloudy triggers). Leave blank to disable.
data_description.solcast_enabled: Enable solar production forecasting. If the Solcast HA integration is installed, PowerSync reads from its sensors automatically (no API calls needed).
data_description.solcast_api_key: Only needed if you do NOT have the Solcast HA integration. Get a free key from toolkit.solcast.com.au (10 calls/day on hobbyist tier).
data_description.solcast_resource_id: Only needed without the Solcast HA integration. For split arrays, enter comma-separated IDs (e.g. abc123,def456).

demand_charges.title: Demand charge tracking
demand_charges.description: Track peak demand during capacity-based billing periods. Leave unchecked to skip if your plan has no demand charges.

data.demand_allow_grid_charging: Allow grid charging during demand windows

data_description.demand_charge_enabled: Track your peak grid draw during demand charge periods and calculate monthly capacity fees.
data_description.demand_charge_rate: Your network demand charge rate in dollars per kW of peak demand. Check your bill for this value.
data_description.demand_charge_start_time: Start time of the demand charge measurement period (24-hour format).
data_description.demand_charge_end_time: End time of the demand charge measurement period (24-hour format).
data_description.demand_allow_grid_charging: Allow battery grid charging during demand peak periods. Disable to protect against demand charge spikes.

curtailment_setup.title: Negative FIT curtailment
curtailment_setup.description: When feed-in prices go negative, you pay to export. These settings stop battery export and optionally shut down solar.

data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting to grid when feed-in price is negative. This is the main toggle -- must be enabled for any curtailment to work.
data_description.ac_inverter_curtailment_enabled: Shut down AC-coupled solar inverter (Fronius, Sungrow, GoodWe, Enphase) when feed-in price is negative. Use if solar bypasses your battery and exports directly.

inverter_brand_setup.title: AC-coupled inverter brand
inverter_brand_setup.description: Select your solar inverter brand for direct curtailment control during negative feed-in prices.

data_description.inverter_brand: The brand of your AC-coupled solar inverter. PowerSync uses brand-specific protocols for shutdown commands.

inverter_config_setup.title: {brand} inverter configuration
inverter_config_setup.description: Configure the connection to your {brand} inverter for curtailment control.

data_description.inverter_host: Local IP address of your inverter or gateway.
data_description.inverter_port: 502 for Modbus (Sungrow, Fronius, GoodWe, Huawei), 443 for Enphase.
data_description.inverter_token: Optional if using Enlighten credentials. Manual tokens expire in 12 hours for installer accounts.
data_description.enphase_username: Your Enlighten account email for automatic JWT token refresh (recommended).
data_description.enphase_serial: Leave blank to auto-detect from IQ Gateway.
data_description.enphase_normal_profile: Full name of your normal grid profile. Example: 'AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Australia A'.
data_description.enphase_zero_export_profile: Full name of a zero-export grid profile if available (for fallback curtailment).
data_description.enphase_is_installer: Enable if using an installer Enlighten account. Required for grid profile switching.
data_description.inverter_restore_soc: Restore inverter when battery drops below this %. Ensures battery stays topped up before evening peak. Default: 98%.
data_description.fronius_load_following: Enable if you do NOT have a 0W export limit. Uses calculated power limits based on home load. Leave off if you have a 0W export profile (most users).

battery_management_mode.title: Battery management mode
battery_management_mode.description: Choose how PowerSync manages your battery.

data_description.battery_management_mode: Manual = PowerSync automations control charge/discharge. TOU Sync = prices synced to battery's built-in scheduler. Smart Optimization = LP solver minimises costs.
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not discharge below this level. Separate from your battery's hardware backup reserve (set in the mobile app).

solax_battery.title: Solax Hybrid connection
solax_battery.description: PowerSync bridges through the homeassistant-solax-modbus integration. Install it from HACS (search 'Solax Modbus') and configure your inverter, then return here.

data_description.solax_entity_prefix: The entity prefix used by the solax_modbus integration (e.g. 'solax' → sensor.solax_battery_capacity). Check Developer Tools > States if unsure.
data_description.solax_battery_capacity_kwh: Usable battery capacity in kWh. Used by the LP optimizer.
data_description.solax_battery_nominal_v: Nominal battery voltage in V (51.2 for LFP T-BAT, 102.4 for high-voltage packs).
data_description.solax_max_charge_current_a: Maximum charge current your battery supports (A).
data_description.solax_max_discharge_current_a: Maximum discharge current your battery supports (A).

error.invalid_auth: Token rejected by the API -- check you copied the full token string with no extra spaces.
error.cannot_connect: Cannot reach the API server. Check your internet connection and that Home Assistant has outbound HTTPS access.
error.no_sites: No Amber sites found for this token. Verify the token belongs to an account with an active Amber site.
error.no_energy_sites: No Tesla energy sites found. Ensure your Powerwall or Solar system is set up in the Tesla app before continuing.
error.no_token_provided: Paste a token in the field above. See the hint text for where to find it.
error.tesla_fleet_not_installed: Tesla Fleet integration is not installed in Home Assistant. Add it from Settings > Devices & Services > Add Integration > Tesla Fleet, then return here.
error.aemo_region_required: Select your NEM region when spike detection is enabled. Check your electricity bill for your NEM zone.
error.aemo_required_in_aemo_mode: Spike detection is recommended for Globird and VPP plans with spike export payments. Disable only if your plan does not include this feature.
error.missing_credentials: Email and password are both required. Enter your Sigenergy account credentials.
error.invalid_device_id: Device ID must be exactly 13 digits. Leave blank if your account does not require one.
error.no_stations: No Sigenergy stations found. Check that your Sigenergy account has at least one commissioned station.
error.no_station_selected: Select a Sigenergy station from the list to continue.
error.modbus_host_required: IP address is required -- enter the local IP of your Sigenergy inverter or gateway.
error.sungrow_host_required: IP address is required -- enter the local IP of your Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle.
error.modbus_conflict: Connected but received invalid data (SOC > 100%). Another integration is likely using Modbus on the same port. Disable other Modbus integrations for this inverter and retry.
error.sungrow_modbus_conflict: The AC inverter and Sungrow battery share the same IP, port, and slave ID. Change the AC inverter's slave ID to a different value.
error.foxess_host_required: IP address is required -- enter the local IP of your FoxESS inverter.
error.foxess_tcp_failed: Cannot connect via Modbus TCP. Check: (1) IP is correct (H3 Smart shows as 'espressif'), (2) firmware is current, (3) port 502 is reachable from Home Assistant.
error.foxess_serial_required: Serial port path is required -- e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 for a USB-to-RS485 adapter.
error.foxess_cloud_auth_failed: FoxESS Cloud rejected the API key. Verify the key is correct and that API access is enabled on your FoxESS account.
error.foxess_cloud_connection_error: Cannot reach FoxESS Cloud. Check your internet connection and retry.
error.goodwe_connect_failed: Cannot connect to GoodWe inverter. Verify the IP address is correct and the inverter is powered on and reachable from Home Assistant.
error.goodwe_no_battery: This GoodWe model (DT/MS/XS series) does not support battery control. Only ET/EH/ES/EM series hybrid inverters are supported.
error.esy_sunhome_not_installed: The ESY Sunhome integration is not installed. Install it via HACS (search 'ESY Sunhome') and add a device before continuing.
error.esy_sunhome_no_device: No ESY Sunhome device found in the selected integration entry. Verify the ESY Sunhome integration is set up and has a paired device.
error.invalid_credentials: Email or password is incorrect. Check your Flow Power portal login credentials.
error.invalid_mfa_code: Verification code is incorrect or expired. Request a new SMS code and try again.
error.zaptec_auth_failed: Zaptec Cloud login failed. Check that your email and password are correct.
error.zaptec_missing_credentials: Both email and password are required for Zaptec Cloud authentication.
error.no_prices: No prices found for this tariff and region combination. Verify your Octopus product and GSP region match your account.
error.optimizer_not_installed: Smart Optimization requires scipy, which is not installed. Check Home Assistant logs for installation errors.
error.invalid_tariff: This tariff code is not recognised for the selected network distributor. Check the code on your electricity bill.
error.invalid_token_format: Token format is not recognised. PowerSync tokens start with 'psync_' -- make sure you copied the full string.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs for details and try again.
error.solax_missing_entities: Some required solax_modbus entities were not found. Ensure the homeassistant-solax-modbus integration is installed and running, and that the entity prefix matches (check Developer Tools > States for 'sensor.<prefix>_battery_capacity').
error.solax_connect_failed: Failed to validate Solax entities. Check the entity prefix and try again.

abort.already_configured: PowerSync is already configured. To change settings, use the Configure button on the integration card.
abort.no_energy_sites: No Tesla energy sites found. Ensure you have a Powerwall or Solar system set up in the Tesla app before adding PowerSync.
abort.no_stations: No Sigenergy stations found. Check that your credentials are correct and your station is commissioned.
abort.reauth_failed: Re-authentication failed. Remove the integration and set it up again.
abort.reauth_fleet_api_use_tesla_fleet: PowerSync reads tokens from your Tesla Fleet integration. Go to Settings > Devices & Services > Tesla Fleet and re-authenticate there. PowerSync will pick up the new tokens automatically.
abort.esy_sunhome_not_installed: The ESY Sunhome integration is not installed. Install it via HACS (search 'ESY Sunhome') and add a device before continuing.

init.title: PowerSync settings
init.description: Choose a section to configure. Changes take effect immediately.

pricing.title: Electricity pricing
pricing.description: Change your electricity provider or update pricing settings. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.electricity_provider: Changing provider will clear your current pricing configuration and start fresh.

tesla_connection.title: Tesla API connection
tesla_connection.description: Update your Powerwall and vehicle API providers. Changes take effect immediately.

data.update_teslemetry_token: New API token (leave blank to keep current)

data_description.tesla_api_provider: Controls how PowerSync reads and writes your Powerwall TOU schedule.
data_description.tesla_ev_api_provider: Only needed for vehicle commands. Choose None if you only have a Powerwall.
data_description.update_teslemetry_token: Paste a new token to rotate it. Leave blank to keep your existing token.

sigenergy_connection.title: Sigenergy connection
sigenergy_connection.description: Update your Sigenergy Modbus and Cloud API settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.sigenergy_modbus_host: Local IP address of your Sigenergy inverter or gateway.
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_port: Default Modbus TCP port is 502. Only change if customised on the inverter.
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_slave_id: Default is 247. Only change if you have multiple devices on the same bus.
data_description.sigenergy_dc_curtailment_enabled: Reduce DC solar output when export prices go negative. Requires Modbus write access.
data_description.sigenergy_export_limit_kw: Maximum export power in kW. Leave blank for no limit.
data_description.sigenergy_pass_enc: Pre-encoded password from the Sigenergy API. Use only if you know what this is.
data_description.sigenergy_device_id: 13-digit device ID. Leave blank if your account does not require one.

sungrow_connection.title: Sungrow Modbus connection
sungrow_connection.description: Update your Sungrow inverter Modbus settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.sungrow_host: Local IP of your Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sungrow_slave_id: Default is 1. Only change if customised on the inverter.
data_description.sungrow_host_2: Leave blank if you only have one inverter.
data_description.sungrow_grid_inverter_soc_cap: Maximum SOC percentage for grid-connected inverter in dual setups. Default 100 (disabled).
data_description.sungrow_battery_capacity_1: Battery capacity in kWh for the primary inverter. Used for weighted SOC calculation in dual setups.
data_description.sungrow_battery_capacity_2: Battery capacity in kWh for the secondary inverter.

foxess_connection_options.title: FoxESS connection
foxess_connection_options.description: Update your FoxESS inverter Modbus settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.foxess_host: Local IP of your FoxESS inverter. H3 Smart shows as 'espressif' on your network.
data_description.foxess_serial_port: e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 for a USB-to-RS485 adapter. Only used for serial connections.
data_description.foxess_serial_baudrate: Default 9600. Only change if the inverter is configured differently.

goodwe_connection_options.title: GoodWe connection
goodwe_connection_options.description: Update your GoodWe inverter connection settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.goodwe_host: Local IP address of your GoodWe inverter.
data_description.goodwe_protocol: UDP for WiFi dongles (port 8899), TCP for LAN dongles (port 502).

esy_sunhome_connection.title: ESY Sunhome connection
esy_sunhome_connection.description: Update the ESY Sunhome integration entry PowerSync bridges through.

data_description.esy_config_entry_id: The ESY Sunhome integration entry to read sensors and send mode commands through.

optimization.title: Optimization mode
optimization.description: Change how PowerSync schedules your battery. Changes apply after the next optimization cycle.

data_description.optimization_provider: Smart Optimization uses an LP solver to minimise costs. Native mode uses your battery's built-in TOU scheduler.
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not discharge below this percentage. Separate from your battery's hardware backup reserve.

options_tesla_ev_token.title: Vehicle API token
options_tesla_ev_token.description: Teslemetry is not installed in Home Assistant, so PowerSync needs an API token for vehicle commands. Get a token from [{teslemetry_url}]({teslemetry_url}).

data_description.tesla_ev_teslemetry_token: This token is only used for vehicle commands. Your Powerwall uses a separate connection.

init_sigenergy.title: Sigenergy settings
init_sigenergy.description: Configure pricing, optimization, Modbus connection, and Cloud API credentials. Modbus is required for energy data; Cloud API is required for price sync.

data_description.sigenergy_modbus_host: Local IP address of your Sigenergy inverter (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sigenergy_modbus_slave_id: Default is 247. PowerSync auto-detects the correct registers.
data_description.sigenergy_dc_curtailment_enabled: Reduce DC solar output when export prices go negative. Requires Modbus write access.
data_description.sigenergy_export_limit_kw: Your DNSP grid export limit in kW. Leave blank to auto-detect from inverter registers.
data_description.sigenergy_username: Your Sigenergy Cloud account email address.
data_description.sigenergy_password: Your Sigenergy account password (leave blank to keep existing).
data_description.sigenergy_pass_enc: Pre-encoded password -- leave blank unless you know what this is.
data_description.sigenergy_device_id: Optional -- leave blank unless authentication fails without it.

init_sungrow.title: Sungrow settings
init_sungrow.description: Configure pricing, optimization, and Sungrow Modbus connection. Modbus is required for energy data and battery control.

data_description.optimization_provider: Choose between Sungrow's built-in scheduling or Smart Optimization (LP solver).
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not discharge below this level. Separate from your battery's hardware backup reserve.
data_description.sungrow_host: Local IP of your Sungrow inverter or WiNet-S dongle (e.g. 192.168.1.100).
data_description.sungrow_slave_id: Default is 1. Only change if customised.
data_description.sungrow_host_2: IP of a second Sungrow inverter. Leave blank if not applicable.
data_description.sungrow_slave_id_2: Default is 1. Only change if customised.
data_description.sungrow_grid_inverter_soc_cap: Maximum charge level for the grid-forming inverter. Set below 100% to reserve headroom for off-grid buffering (dual inverter setups only).
data_description.sungrow_battery_capacity_1: Total battery capacity connected to the primary inverter in kWh. Used for weighted SOC averaging and power splitting.
data_description.sungrow_battery_capacity_2: Total battery capacity connected to the secondary inverter in kWh.

init_foxess.title: FoxESS settings
init_foxess.description: Configure pricing, optimization, and FoxESS Modbus connection. Modbus is required for energy data and battery control.

data_description.optimization_provider: Choose between FoxESS built-in scheduling or Smart Optimization (LP solver).
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not discharge below this level. Separate from your battery's hardware backup reserve.
data_description.foxess_host: Local IP of your FoxESS inverter. H3 Smart may appear as 'espressif' on your network.
data_description.foxess_serial_port: e.g. /dev/ttyUSB0 -- only for RS485 serial connections.
data_description.foxess_serial_baudrate: Default 9600 -- only for RS485 serial connections.

init_goodwe.title: GoodWe settings
init_goodwe.description: Configure pricing, optimization, and GoodWe connection. Required for energy data and battery control.

data_description.optimization_provider: Choose between GoodWe built-in scheduling or Smart Optimization (LP solver).
data_description.optimization_backup_reserve: The optimizer will not discharge below this level. Separate from your battery's hardware backup reserve.
data_description.goodwe_host: Local IP address of your GoodWe inverter.
data_description.goodwe_protocol: UDP for WiFi dongles (port 8899), TCP for LAN dongles (port 502).

teslemetry_token.title: Teslemetry API token
teslemetry_token.description: Enter your Teslemetry API token from teslemetry.com. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.teslemetry_api_token: Paste the full token string from your Teslemetry dashboard.

powersync_token.title: PowerSync token
powersync_token.description: Sign in at **{auth_url}** with your Tesla account and paste the `psync_` token below. Your Tesla credentials are never shared with PowerSync.

data_description.teslemetry_api_token: Token starting with psync_ from the PowerSync sign-in page.

amber_options.title: Amber Electric options
amber_options.description: Configure TOU sync, export boost, spike protection, and curtailment. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.update_amber_token: Paste a new Amber API token to rotate it. Leave blank to keep your current token.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: Automatically upload TOU tariff schedules to your battery based on current Amber prices.
data_description.amber_forecast_type: Predicted = balanced. Low = assumes cheaper prices (aggressive charging). High = assumes expensive prices (conservative).
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery exports and DC solar when feed-in prices go negative.
data_description.spike_protection_enabled: Artificially inflate buy prices during Amber price spikes to prevent grid charging.
data_description.ac_inverter_curtailment_enabled: Shut down AC-coupled solar inverter when feed-in price is negative. Use if solar bypasses your battery.
data_description.settled_prices_only: Skip forecast sync at :00 and only sync when actual prices arrive at :35/:60. Use when forecasts diverge from settled prices.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_alert: Send a notification when predicted and conservative forecasts diverge significantly.
data_description.forecast_discrepancy_threshold: Average price difference that triggers the alert. Default 10 c/kWh.
data_description.force_tariff_mode_toggle: After uploading a new tariff, briefly toggle operation mode to force recalculation. Tesla only, ~5 second interruption.
data_description.export_boost_enabled: Artificially increase sell prices during configured hours to encourage battery export.
data_description.export_price_offset: Amount added to the actual Amber export price. Example: Amber 18c + Offset 5c = 23c.
data_description.export_min_price: Floor price after offset. Example: Amber 12c + Offset 5c = 17c, bumped to Floor 20c.
data_description.export_boost_threshold: Only boost when actual Amber price is at or above this value. Use 0 to always boost.
data_description.chip_mode_enabled: Suppress exports during configured hours unless price exceeds threshold.
data_description.chip_mode_threshold: Allow exports only when price is at or above this value.

flow_power_options.title: Flow Power settings
flow_power_options.description: Update your Flow Power pricing and portal settings. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.flow_power_price_source: AEMO Direct fetches wholesale prices from NEMWeb (no API key). Amber API uses your Amber Electric account.
data_description.pea_enabled: PEA reflects how Flow Power actually bills you: final rate moves with wholesale prices. Disable for flat network tariff rates.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: Automatically upload TOU tariff schedules to your battery based on current prices.

flow_power_portal_reauth.title: Portal re-authentication
flow_power_portal_reauth.description: Enter your Flow Power portal credentials to reconnect.

data_description.flowpower_email: The email address you use to log in to the Flow Power portal.

flow_power_portal_mfa_options.title: SMS verification
flow_power_portal_mfa_options.description: Enter the SMS code sent to your registered phone number.

data_description.mfa_code: 6-digit code from the SMS sent to your Flow Power registered phone.

flow_power_amber_token.title: Amber API token
flow_power_amber_token.description: Enter your Amber Electric API token. Get one from {amber_url}.

data_description.amber_api_token: Found in the Amber app under Profile > Developer > API key.

flow_power_network_options.title: Flow Power network & tariff
flow_power_network_options.description: Select your network tariff for accurate PEA pricing. Advanced overrides for manual rate entry are at the bottom.

data_description.fp_network_tariff_combined: Your DNSP and tariff code (e.g. 'Ausgrid — RESELE'). Filtered by your NEM region. Select None for the simple formula.
data_description.fp_twap_override: Override the dynamic 30-day TWAP with a fixed value. Set to 0 for automatic rolling average.
data_description.fp_amber_markup: Retailer margin for the Amber comparison sensor. Regional default is pre-filled.
data_description.pea_custom_value: Override the calculated PEA with a fixed amount. Set to 0 for automatic calculation.
data_description.network_use_manual_rates: Enter network tariff rates manually instead of using the automatic lookup.
data_description.network_other_fees: Additional per-kWh fees not covered by the network tariff (metering, environmental charges, etc.).
data_description.network_include_gst: Add 10% GST on top of the network tariff rates.

globird_options.title: Globird settings
globird_options.description:
Configure your Globird plan and optional AEMO spike export. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

{tariff_hint}

data_description.aemo_spike_enabled: Enable if your Globird plan includes payments for exporting during AEMO price spikes.
data_description.aemo_region: Your NEM region. Check your electricity bill if unsure.
data_description.aemo_spike_threshold: Battery exports when the AEMO wholesale price exceeds this value. Default $3,000/MWh.
data_description.configure_custom_tariff: Recommended: TOU rates are needed for accurate price sensors, optimization, and EV charging.

localvolts_options.title: Localvolts settings
localvolts_options.description: Update your Localvolts credentials and sync settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.localvolts_api_key: Your Localvolts API key. Keep it secret -- treat it like a password.
data_description.localvolts_nmi: Your 10-digit National Meter Identifier. Use * for all authorized NMIs.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: Automatically upload TOU tariff schedules to your battery based on current prices.
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting when prices are negative to avoid paying to export.

epex_options.title: EPEX day-ahead settings
epex_options.description: Update your EPEX pricing settings. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.epex_region: Your EPEX bidding zone. Most users need just their country code.
data_description.epex_surcharge: Fixed network fees and levies added to wholesale price before tax.
data_description.epex_tax_percent: VAT percentage. Example: 21 for Belgium, 19 for Germany.
data_description.epex_export_rate: Fixed feed-in tariff in ct/kWh. Set to 0 to use wholesale price.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: Automatically upload TOU tariff schedules to your battery.
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting when prices go negative.

octopus_options.title: Octopus Energy settings
octopus_options.description: Update your Octopus tariff and sync settings. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.octopus_product: Agile = half-hourly dynamic. Go/Intelligent Go = cheap overnight. Flux/Intelligent Flux = solar+battery optimised. Tracker = daily wholesale.
data_description.octopus_region: Your Grid Supply Point region (single letter A-P). Check your Octopus bill.
data_description.auto_sync_enabled: Automatically upload TOU tariff schedules to your battery based on current prices.
data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting when prices go negative.

octopus_saving_sessions_options.title: Saving sessions settings
octopus_saving_sessions_options.description: Configure automatic battery export during Octopus Saving Sessions. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_enabled: Enable automatic battery export during Octopus Saving Sessions.
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_source: Direct uses Octopus GraphQL API (requires API key). Entity reads from Bottlecap Dave's integration.
data_description.octopus_api_key: Your Octopus API key from the developer dashboard.
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_entity: Entity ID of the Bottlecap Dave saving sessions event entity.
data_description.octopus_saving_sessions_auto_join: Automatically join available sessions. Only works in direct mode.

nz_options.title: NZ electricity settings
nz_options.description: Update your NZ electricity plan and rates. All values in NZD cents per kWh. Changes apply after the next sync cycle.

data_description.nz_retailer: Select your NZ retailer. Rates will update to match the selected plan.
data_description.nz_shoulder_rate: Weekday daytime and weekend daytime rate.
data_description.nz_daily_supply: Fixed daily supply charge from your retailer.

data_description.plan_name: Optional label for this tariff plan.
data_description.tariff_type: Flat rate uses a single price all day. Time of Use lets you add multiple periods.
data_description.flat_rate: Only used for flat rate tariff type.
data_description.offpeak_rate: Rate for hours not covered by any defined period.
data_description.fit_rate: Default export rate for off-peak hours.

data_description.period_type: e.g. Peak, Shoulder, Off-Peak, Super Off-Peak.
data_description.import_rate: What you pay per kWh to import during this period.
data_description.export_rate: What you earn per kWh for exporting during this period.
data_description.add_another: Uncheck when done. Remaining hours become off-peak.

curtailment_options.title: Curtailment settings
curtailment_options.description: Configure automatic curtailment during negative feed-in prices. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.battery_curtailment_enabled: Stop battery from exporting when feed-in price is negative. This is the main toggle -- must be enabled for any curtailment to work.
data_description.ac_inverter_curtailment_enabled: Shut down AC-coupled solar inverter when feed-in price is negative. Use if solar bypasses your battery. Requires inverter configuration.
data_description.sigenergy_dc_curtailment_enabled: Limit DC solar production via Sigenergy Modbus when feed-in price is negative. Only for Sigenergy systems with DC-coupled solar.
data_description.openweathermap_api_key: Free key from openweathermap.org for weather-based automations. Leave blank to disable.

demand_charge_options.title: Demand charge tracking
demand_charge_options.description: Track peak demand during capacity-based billing periods. Changes take effect immediately.

data.demand_allow_grid_charging: Allow grid charging during demand windows

data_description.demand_charge_enabled: Track your peak grid draw during demand periods and calculate monthly capacity fees.
data_description.demand_charge_rate: Your network demand charge rate in $/kW of peak demand. Check your bill.
data_description.demand_charge_start_time: Start of the demand charge measurement period (24-hour format).
data_description.demand_charge_end_time: End of the demand charge measurement period (24-hour format).
data_description.demand_charge_days: Which days demand charges apply (weekdays, all days, etc.).
data_description.demand_charge_billing_day: Day of month when the billing cycle resets (1-28).
data_description.demand_charge_apply_to: Whether demand charges apply to grid imports, exports, or both.
data_description.demand_artificial_price_enabled: Add an artificial price component during demand peak periods to discourage grid draw.
data_description.demand_allow_grid_charging: Allow battery grid charging during demand peak periods. Disable to protect against demand charge spikes.
data_description.daily_supply_charge: Your daily electricity supply charge for cost tracking.
data_description.monthly_supply_charge: Your monthly electricity supply charge for cost tracking.

ev_charging_setup.title: EV charging setup
ev_charging_setup.description: Configure EV charger control. Each section is independent -- only enable the charger(s) that match your setup.

data_description.ev_charging_enabled: Enable smart charging for Tesla vehicles. Not required for OCPP or Zaptec chargers.
data_description.ev_provider: Fleet API/Teslemetry = cloud API. Tesla BLE = local Bluetooth via ESPHome. Both = BLE with Fleet API fallback.
data_description.tesla_ble_entity_prefix: Must match your ESPHome device name(s). For multiple vehicles: my_tesla, wifes_tesla. Default: tesla_ble.
data_description.ocpp_enabled: Run an OCPP 1.6J WebSocket server for OCPP-compatible chargers. Chargers connect to ws://your-ha-ip:port/charger_id.
data_description.ocpp_port: Port for the OCPP WebSocket server. Default: 9000.
data_description.zaptec_charger_entity: Entity ID from the Zaptec HA integration (e.g. switch.zaptec_charger). Leave blank if using standalone mode.
data_description.zaptec_installation_id: Installation device ID for current limiting. Found in the Zaptec HA integration device list. Leave blank to auto-detect.
data_description.zaptec_standalone_enabled: Connect directly to Zaptec Cloud API without the HA integration. Credentials are entered on the next page.
data_description.generic_charger_enabled: Control any charger with a switch entity in Home Assistant (e.g. Evnex, Wallbox).
data_description.generic_charger_switch_entity: Entity ID of the switch that starts/stops charging (e.g. switch.evnex_charge_now). Required.
data_description.generic_charger_amps_entity: Entity ID for charge current control in amps. Leave blank if not supported.
data_description.generic_charger_status_entity: Entity ID reporting connector status. Recognised: charging, preparing, suspended_evse, suspended_ev, finishing. Leave blank to skip.
data_description.generic_charger_soc_entity: Entity ID reporting EV battery SoC as a percentage. Used for target SoC charging. Leave blank if unavailable.

zaptec_cloud.title: Zaptec Cloud login
zaptec_cloud.description: Enter your Zaptec account credentials. PowerSync will auto-discover your chargers and installations.

data_description.zaptec_username: Your Zaptec account email (same as Zaptec Portal login).

zaptec_cloud_options.title: Zaptec Cloud credentials
zaptec_cloud_options.description: Update your Zaptec account credentials. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.zaptec_username: Your Zaptec account email (same as Zaptec Portal login).

ev_charging.title: EV charging settings
ev_charging.description: Update your EV charger configuration. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.ev_charging_enabled: Enable smart charging for Tesla vehicles. Not required for OCPP or Zaptec chargers.
data_description.ev_provider: Fleet API/Teslemetry = cloud API. Tesla BLE = local Bluetooth via ESPHome. Both = BLE with Fleet API fallback.
data_description.tesla_ble_entity_prefix: Must match your ESPHome device name(s). For multiple vehicles: my_tesla, wifes_tesla. Default: tesla_ble.
data_description.ocpp_enabled: Run an OCPP 1.6J WebSocket server for OCPP-compatible chargers. Chargers connect to ws://your-ha-ip:port/charger_id.
data_description.ocpp_port: Port for the OCPP WebSocket server. Default: 9000.
data_description.zaptec_charger_entity: Entity ID from the Zaptec HA integration (e.g. switch.zaptec_charger). Leave blank if using standalone mode.
data_description.zaptec_installation_id: Installation device ID for current limiting. Leave blank to auto-detect.
data_description.zaptec_standalone_enabled: Connect directly to Zaptec Cloud API without the HA integration.
data_description.generic_charger_enabled: Control any charger with a switch entity in Home Assistant.
data_description.generic_charger_switch_entity: Entity ID of the switch that starts/stops charging. Required.
data_description.generic_charger_amps_entity: Entity ID for charge current control in amps. Leave blank if not supported.
data_description.generic_charger_status_entity: Entity ID reporting connector status. Leave blank to skip plug detection.
data_description.generic_charger_soc_entity: Entity ID reporting EV battery SoC as a percentage. Leave blank if unavailable.

inverter_brand.title: AC-coupled inverter brand
inverter_brand.description: Select your solar inverter brand for curtailment control during negative feed-in prices.

data_description.inverter_brand: The brand of your AC-coupled solar inverter. PowerSync uses brand-specific protocols for shutdown commands.

inverter_config.title: {brand} inverter configuration
inverter_config.description: Update your {brand} inverter connection settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.inverter_host: Local IP address of your inverter or gateway.
data_description.inverter_port: 502 for Modbus (Sungrow, Fronius, GoodWe, Huawei), 443 for Enphase.
data_description.inverter_token: Optional if using Enlighten credentials. Manual tokens expire in 12 hours for installer accounts.
data_description.enphase_username: Your Enlighten account email for automatic JWT token refresh (recommended).
data_description.enphase_serial: Leave blank to auto-detect from IQ Gateway.
data_description.enphase_normal_profile: Full name of your normal grid profile. Example: 'AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 Australia A'.
data_description.enphase_zero_export_profile: Full name of a zero-export grid profile if available.
data_description.enphase_is_installer: Enable if using an installer Enlighten account. Required for grid profile switching.
data_description.inverter_restore_soc: Restore inverter when battery drops below this %. Default: 98%.
data_description.fronius_load_following: Enable if you do NOT have a 0W export limit. Uses calculated power limits. Leave off if you have a 0W export profile (most users).

weather_options.title: Weather & solar forecast
weather_options.description: Update weather automations and solar forecasting settings. Changes take effect immediately.

data_description.weather_location: Suburb, city, postcode, or lat,lon coordinates (e.g. 'Melbourne', '3000', '-37.81,144.96'). Leave blank to auto-detect.
data_description.openweathermap_api_key: Free key from openweathermap.org for weather-based automations. Leave blank to disable.
data_description.solcast_enabled: Enable solar forecasting. If the Solcast HA integration is installed, PowerSync reads from its sensors automatically.
data_description.solcast_api_key: Only needed without the Solcast HA integration. Get a free key from toolkit.solcast.com.au.
data_description.solcast_resource_id: Only needed without the Solcast HA integration. For split arrays, enter comma-separated IDs.

solax_battery_options.title: Solax connection settings
solax_battery_options.description: Update the Solax Modbus integration entity prefix and battery parameters.

data_description.solax_entity_prefix: The entity prefix used by the solax_modbus integration.
data_description.solax_battery_nominal_v: Nominal battery voltage in V (51.2 for LFP, 102.4 for HV packs).

sync_tou_schedule.name: Sync TOU Schedule
sync_tou_schedule.description: Manually trigger a sync of Time-of-Use schedule from Amber to Tesla

sync_now.name: Sync Now
sync_now.description: Immediately refresh data from Amber and Tesla
